ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat/ALPHV pre-encryption Safe Mode reboot via bcdedit + ransomware service install
ucs.description: Hunts BlackCat (ALPHV/Noberus) operators staging encryption by booting the host into Safe Mode (where most EDR/AV agents do not load) and registering a service that re-runs the ransomware on reboot. The chained sequence — bcdedit safeboot + new auto-start service within minutes — is documented Microsoft/Mandiant tradecraft for this RaaS crew and is not seen in legitimate IT workflows.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe Processes.process="*safeboot*" (Processes.process="*network*" OR Processes.process="*minimal*")) OR (Processes.process_name=vssadmin.exe Processes.process="*delete shadows*") OR (Processes.process_name=wmic.exe Processes.process="*shadowcopy*" Processes.process="*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe,pwsh.exe) Processes.process="*Win32_Shadowcopy*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval indicator=case(process_name="bcdedit.exe","safeboot",process_name="vssadmin.exe" OR process_name="wmic.exe" OR like(process_name,"%powershell%"),"vss_delete") | stats min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(process) as cmds values(parent_process_name) as parents dc(indicator) as indicator_types values(indicator) as observed_indicators by host user | where indicator_types>=2 AND mvfind(observed_indicators,"safeboot")>=0 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 1800;
let SafeBoot = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot"
       and (ProcessCommandLine has "network" or ProcessCommandLine has "minimal")
    | project SafeBootTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              BcdeditCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              BcdeditParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
let ServiceInstall = DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType in ("ServiceInstalled", "PowerShellCommand")
    | project SvcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, ServiceFile = FileName,
              ServiceFolder = FolderPath, ServiceCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              SvcAccount = InitiatingProcessAccountName, AdditionalFields;
let VssDelete = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
         or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "Win32_Shadowcopy")
    | project VssTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, VssCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
SafeBoot
| join kind=inner ServiceInstall on DeviceId
| where SvcTime between (SafeBootTime - WindowSec * 1s .. SafeBootTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| join kind=leftouter VssDelete on DeviceId
| where isnull(VssTime) or VssTime between (SafeBootTime - 1h .. SafeBootTime + 1h)
| project SafeBootTime, DeviceName, AccountName, BcdeditCmd, BcdeditParent,
          ServiceFile, ServiceFolder, ServiceCmd, VssCmd
| order by SafeBootTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 1800;
let SafeBoot = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\bcdedit.exe"
    | where CommandLine has "safeboot"
       and (CommandLine has "network" or CommandLine has "minimal")
    | project SafeBootTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              BcdeditCmd = CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
let ServiceInstall = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4697
    | project SvcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, ServiceName, ServiceFileName,
              ServiceStartType, ServiceAccount, SubjectUserName;
let VssDelete = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where (NewProcessName endswith @"\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith @"\wmic.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
         or (NewProcessName has_any (@"\powershell.exe",@"\pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has "Win32_Shadowcopy")
    | project VssTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, VssCmd = CommandLine;
SafeBoot
| join kind=inner ServiceInstall on Computer
| where SvcTime between (SafeBootTime - WindowSec * 1s .. SafeBootTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| join kind=leftouter VssDelete on Computer
| where isnull(VssTime) or VssTime between (SafeBootTime - 1h .. SafeBootTime + 1h)
| project SafeBootTime, Computer, Account, BcdeditCmd, ParentProcessName,
          ServiceName, ServiceFileName, ServiceStartType, VssCmd
| order by SafeBootTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BlackCat/ALPHV Safe Mode Boot Configuration via bcdedit
id: 9c1f8c3e-7e4a-4b1f-9e7d-d3f7a6c8f201
status: experimental
description: Detects bcdedit.exe being used to set the system to boot into Safe Mode (network or minimal). Heavily abused by BlackCat/ALPHV ransomware to disable EDR before staging encryption.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/06/13/the-many-lives-of-blackcat-ransomware/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/blackcat-ransomware-affiliates
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1562.009
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\bcdedit.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'safeboot'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' network'
      - ' minimal'
  condition: selection_img and selection_cmd
falsepositives:
  - Authorized recovery / troubleshooting by IT (extremely rare in production)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft (DEV-0237/DEV-0504), Mandiant and Sophos all attribute the 'reboot into Safe Mode then run the ransomware as a service' pattern to BlackCat/ALPHV affiliates — the Rust-based encryptor was specifically engineered to operate in Safe Mode where Defender/CrowdStrike/SentinelOne sensors are not loaded. The chain (bcdedit safeboot network → new auto-start service → optional VSS purge) is uniqu

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat ExMatter exfiltration: temp-staged binary opening SFTP/22 to public host
ucs.description: Hunts BlackCat's signature pre-encryption data-theft tool ExMatter (also called Fendr / Eamfo). ExMatter is a .NET/Rust binary the operators drop in C:\ProgramData, %TEMP%, or C:\Users\Public, recursively reads office/PDF/source-code files, and exfiltrates them via SFTP (port 22) — sometimes WebDAV/HTTPS — to attacker-controlled staging servers before encryption.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count earliest(_time) as firstTime latest(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ips dc(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as distinct_dst from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port IN (22,443) NOT (All_Traffic.dest_ip=10.0.0.0/8 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=172.16.0.0/12 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=192.168.0.0/16) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_path All_Traffic.process_name All_Traffic.dest_port host | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | where (like(process_path,"%\\Users\\Public\\%") OR like(process_path,"%\\ProgramData\\%") OR like(process_path,"%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%") OR like(process_path,"%\\Windows\\Temp\\%")) | where (dest_port=22) OR (dest_port=443 AND count>=50 AND distinct_dst<=3) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let StagingPaths = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\",
                            @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Windows\Temp\",
                            @"\PerfLogs\"]);
let StagedBins = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName endswith ".exe"
    | where FolderPath has_any (StagingPaths)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe", "trustedinstaller.exe",
                                            "setup.exe", "OfficeClickToRun.exe")
    | project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ProcessId, BinPath = FolderPath, BinName = FileName, BinSHA256 = SHA256;
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where RemotePort in (22, 443, 8443)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe",
                                         "OneDrive.exe","Teams.exe","outlook.exe",
                                         "svchost.exe","wininit.exe","git.exe","ssh.exe")
| join kind=inner StagedBins
    on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.ProcessId
| where Timestamp between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + WindowMin)
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            DistinctRemoteIPs = dcount(RemoteIP),
            FirstConn = min(Timestamp),
            LastConn = max(Timestamp),
            SampleRemoteIPs = make_set(RemoteIP, 8),
            SampleRemoteUrls = make_set(RemoteUrl, 8)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, BinName, BinPath, BinSHA256, RemotePort
| where (RemotePort == 22 and ConnCount >= 1)
     or (RemotePort in (443, 8443) and ConnCount >= 50 and DistinctRemoteIPs <= 3)
| order by FirstConn desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let StagingPaths = dynamic([@"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\",
                            @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Windows\Temp\",
                            @"\PerfLogs\"]);
let StagedBins = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where NewProcessName endswith ".exe"
    | where NewProcessName has_any (StagingPaths)
    | project ProcTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ProcessId = NewProcessId, BinPath = NewProcessName;
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where DstPortNumber in (22, 443, 8443)
| where ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr) == false
| where ActingProcessName !has_any ("msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe",
                                     "OneDrive.exe","Teams.exe","outlook.exe",
                                     "git.exe","ssh.exe")
| join kind=inner StagedBins on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + WindowMin)
| where ActingProcessName =~ BinPath
| summarize ConnCount = count(),
            DistinctRemoteIPs = dcount(DstIpAddr),
            FirstConn = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastConn = max(TimeGenerated),
            SampleRemoteIPs = make_set(DstIpAddr, 8)
            by Computer, Account, BinPath, DstPortNumber
| where (DstPortNumber == 22 and ConnCount >= 1)
     or (DstPortNumber in (443, 8443) and ConnCount >= 50 and DistinctRemoteIPs <= 3)
| order by FirstConn desc
ucs.rationale: ExMatter (also tracked as Fendr/Eamfo) is uniquely BlackCat/ALPHV — Symantec, Kroll and Cisco Talos all link the .NET-then-Rust exfiltrator exclusively to ALPHV affiliates (and to the ALPHV → BlackByte/Cicada3301 splinter that reused the codebase). The combination of (a) an unsigned binary executing from a non-program staging directory and (b) opening direct SFTP to a public host within minutes is

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, SecurityEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
